_CRITICAL: Disqualifiers (DQ1-DQ7) MUST be implemented as explicit rule modules that run BEFORE score aggregation.
They are NOT soft weights to absorb into math. The JD text is explicit on these — a judge re-reading the JD against your output WILL check for them.
Semantic/embedding match is a SUPPORTING signal, not the primary driver. The JD warns against keyword stuffing as its first hackathon note.
Production evidence from career_history[].description is the highest-weight single signal.

experience._note: Band is a guide, not a hard filter. Override with strong signals elsewhere. Do NOT hard-reject on years alone.

location._note: Outside India is NOT an auto-reject — it is a real headwind. Represent in score, not as a disqualifier. Example: strong Toronto candidate still in scope but scored down.

relocation_bonus._note: Add to location score only if candidate is NOT already in preferred/welcome city.

notice_period._note: 30+ day notice is still in scope but the bar gets higher per JD.

required_skills._meta: All four are critical. Missing any one is a strong negative signal — not just a weight reduction. Required skills have a floor: if a candidate scores 0 on two or more of these, cap total score at 0.40 regardless of other components.

python_code_quality._note: JD says 'yes really, we care about code quality'. This is not just skill presence.

quality_signal_bonus._note: Additive bonuses, capped at +0.10 total on this component.

ranking_evaluation_frameworks._note: This is harder to fake than skill keywords — look for it in career_history descriptions, not just skills[].

preferred_skills._meta: Positive signals, not requirements. Diminishing returns: count only the top 3 matches when aggregating. A candidate with 6 preferred skills should NOT double-score a candidate with 3.

oss_external_validation._note: Cannot reliably detect from text alone. Use github_activity_score and certifications as the primary proxy. Text keywords are secondary.

_CRITICAL: Implement each DQ as an independent rule module that runs BEFORE final score aggregation.
DQ modules take candidate -> (is_triggered: bool, severity: enum, multiplier: float).
Hard rejects set final_score = 0.0 and skip all further stages.
Soft rejects apply score_multiplier AFTER base scoring is complete.
Do NOT combine DQ logic with skill scoring math — keep them separate so each is auditable.

DQ1_pure_research_career.trigger_condition: ALL THREE signals must fire together — do not hard-reject on any single signal alone

signals.A: ALL career_history[].title values contain 'Research', 'Researcher', 'Scientist', or employer is a university/national lab
signals.B: ZERO occurrence of production-evidence verbs across ALL career_history[].description: deployed, shipped, production, launched, served [N] users, live system
signals.C: ALL career_history[].company are universities, academic labs, or explicit research institutes (not product companies)

DQ1_pure_research_career._note: One research role inside a product-company career is NOT this. Check the ENTIRE career, not just current/recent.

DQ2_shallow_ai_experience.description: AI experience = only recent (<12mo) LangChain/OpenAI wrapper projects with no pre-LLM-era ML production history

signals.A: All career_history entries containing ML/AI descriptions have start_date after 2022-06-01
signals.B: AI descriptions mention only framework wrappers (LangChain, ChatGPT API, OpenAI API, GPT-4) without retrieval/ranking/recommendation architecture depth
signals.C: No evidence anywhere of pre-LLM-era production ML: recommendation engines, ranking systems, search pipelines, embedding models, NDCG/MRR optimization

DQ2_shallow_ai_experience.override: ANY single pre-2022 ML production deployment mention negates DQ2 entirely

DQ3_non_coding_senior.description: Senior title with 18+ months of no individual code contribution evidence
DQ3_non_coding_senior.trigger_condition: BOTH signals must fire on the CURRENT or MOST RECENT role

A.title_pattern: Current/most recent title matches any of:

signals.B: Description of current/most recent role contains ONLY leadership/coordination verbs (led, defined, mentored, planned, strategized, reviewed, managed) with ZERO individual technical contribution verbs (built, implemented, wrote, coded, shipped, deployed, trained, fine-tuned)

DQ3_non_coding_senior._note: Apply ONLY to current/recent role. A candidate who was EM for 2 years but before that shipped production ML is still viable — the prior ML work counts.

DQ4_title_inflation_hopper.description: Title escalation (Junior→SDE→Senior→Staff→Principal) with <18 months per promoted rung
DQ4_title_inflation_hopper.trigger_condition: TWO OR MORE consecutive upward title promotions each with duration_months < 18
_IMPORTANT: This is NOT generic job-hopping detection.
A lateral move (same title, different company) does NOT trigger this.
Only UPWARD title promotions with short tenure trigger this.
Check the difference in title hierarchy level, not just company switches.

DQ5_pure_consulting_background.description: Entire career at IT services/outsourcing firms with zero product-company experience
DQ5_pure_consulting_background.trigger_condition: ALL career_history[].company map to the consulting/services tier AND no override condition fires
DQ5_pure_consulting_background._note: Cross-check against company_tiers.yaml `it_services_tier1` and `it_services_tier2` lists for the authoritative source.
override_conditions: ANY single career_history entry at a product company (product_tier1, product_tier2, product_tier3_growth, saas_b2b, ai_native, gcc_captive from company_tiers.yaml)
Strong ownership verbs in consulting role descriptions: 'shipped', 'owned end-to-end', 'built from scratch', 'deployed to production', 'I designed and built'
DQ5_pure_consulting_background._note_on_overrides: JD says: 'currently at one of these but prior product-company experience = fine.' Check ENTIRE career, not just current company.

DQ6_wrong_primary_domain.description: Primary expertise is CV/speech/robotics with no cross-domain NLP/IR exposure
DQ6_wrong_primary_domain.trigger_logic: IF (count(cv_speech_robotics_signals in full profile) / count(all_ml_signals) > 0.65) AND (count(nlp_ir_salvage_signals) == 0) THEN trigger
DQ6_wrong_primary_domain._note: The domain check should scan BOTH skills[] array AND career_history[].description. A CV engineer who spent 2 years on NLP retrieval is salvageable.

DQ7_closed_source_only.description: 5+ years entirely on closed-source proprietary systems with zero external validation

signals.B: certifications array is empty OR has fewer than 2 entries
signals.C: No external validation keywords in ANY field: 'paper', 'publication', 'open source', 'OSS', 'GitHub', 'conference', 'blog', 'talk', 'speaker', 'arxiv', 'NeurIPS', 'ICML', 'ACL', 'EMNLP', 'SIGIR'

DQ7_closed_source_only._note: This DQ is probabilistic, not deterministic. Three weak proxies together, not a hard signal. Weight accordingly — treat as a score multiplier of 0.30, not a hard reject. Only apply to product-company candidates (consulting candidates already penalized by DQ5).

production_evidence_keyword_taxonomy._meta: This taxonomy drives career_history[].description mining. It is the MOST IMPORTANT scoring signal in the pipeline. A candidate whose description contains Tier A phrases is more valuable than one whose skills[] array matches perfectly.

negative_evidence._note: Negative evidence from description does NOT negate required skill presence in skills[]. It only reduces the description mining component score.

honeypot_detection._meta: Honeypots are forced to relevance tier 0 in ground truth. >10% in top 100 = Stage 3 disqualification. Your system should naturally avoid them with good production evidence scoring, but add explicit checks to be safe.

checks.name: experience_vs_career_sum
checks._note: Dataset confirmed: one candidate claims 3.0 years but career sums to 61 months. Flag anything where claimed exp exceeds career sum by more than 10%.

checks.name: skill_duration_vs_career_total
checks._note: A skill duration cannot exceed the total career length.

checks.name: expert_skill_zero_duration
checks._note: 'Expert' proficiency with zero months used is a strong contradiction.

checks.name: company_founding_year_tenure
checks.data_source: company_tiers.yaml or external lookup if founding_year known
checks._note: Only applicable for companies where founding year is known. Cannot check for fictional employers — they correctly fall to unclassified.

checks.name: age_proxy_vs_experience
checks.formula: IF education[0].end_year is known: years_of_experience > (current_year - education[0].end_year) + 2
checks._note: +2 year buffer for gap years, late graduation, etc. Be conservative.

honeypot_detection.action: Flag candidate as honeypot. Set score = 0.0. Log reason. Do NOT include in top 100.
honeypot_detection.disqualification_threshold: More than 9 honeypots in your final top-100 = contest disqualification at Stage 3.

title_sieve._meta: Run this FIRST, before any other computation. O(N) string matching. Eliminates ~70,000 of 100,000 candidates in milliseconds. No embeddings, no NLP, just exact/substring match.

hard_eliminate.action: Set score = 0.0. Skip all downstream stages entirely.

conditional_pass.action: Pass to Stage 2 with base_score_penalty = -0.10. REQUIRE career_history description mining to surface any ML work before assigning credit.
conditional_pass._note: These are the ~26K 'hidden gem' candidates. Title alone cannot rank them — description mining decides.

direct_pass_with_scrutiny.action: Pass with neutral base score. Apply all DQ checks. This is where the ~900 ML/AI title candidates land.

soft_red_flag_titles.action: Pass with base_score_penalty = -0.10 AND mandatory DQ6 (wrong_primary_domain) check. Do NOT reward title-domain match before DQ6 clears.
soft_red_flag_titles._note: 153 AI Research Engineers and 132 Computer Vision Engineers in pool. Both are explicitly called out as soft red flags in the JD.

_meta: Apply as a MULTIPLICATIVE modifier on the technical score, not as an additive component.
JD explicitly says: 'Down-weight inactive/unresponsive candidates even if perfect on paper.' This is lifted directly from JD text.

relevant_skill_assessment_scores.value: average of redrob_signals.skill_assessment_scores values for ML/Python/DS/AI keys only
relevant_skill_assessment_scores._note: Ignore assessments for non-relevant skills (e.g. Excel, PowerPoint). Only score AI/ML/Python/DS assessments.

github_activity_score._note: github_activity_score = -1 means no GitHub linked. Treat as 0.0, NOT as a negative. Missing data ≠ bad data.

notice_period_integration._note: Apply notice period scoring from the notice_period section as a separate multiplicative factor AFTER behavioral multiplier.

scoring_architecture._meta: Recommended component breakdown. behavioral_availability is NOT a component — it is a multiplier applied AFTER summing the five components below.

production_evidence_score._note: This is the highest single weight component because it is the hardest to fake and most predictive per JD.

required_skill_match.source: Weighted match of 4 required skills against skills[] array AND career_history description keywords
required_skill_match._note: Do not double-count: if 'NDCG' appears in both skills[] and description, count it once. Max weight is skills[] proficiency + endorsements + duration_months. Description match gives partial credit.

career_trajectory_quality.source: company_tiers.yaml tier weights * fraction of career at product companies; ownership-verb evidence; DQ5 check result; title progression pattern
career_trajectory_quality._note: This component penalizes DQ4/DQ5 candidates via multiplier, not by zeroing them.

preferred_skill_match.source: Top 3 matching preferred skills only (diminishing returns beyond 3)
preferred_skill_match._note: Capped at 3 to prevent keyword stuffers with long skill lists from gaming this.

education_pedigree.source: education[].tier (tier_1 > tier_2 > tier_3 > tier_4) + field_of_study bonus for CS/Math/Statistics/EE
education_pedigree._note: Lowest weight deliberately. Strong production evidence from a tier_3 institution > weak profile from a tier_1 institution.

disqualifier_application.order: Compute final_score first, THEN apply DQ multipliers
disqualifier_application.multiple_DQs: Multiply all triggered DQ multipliers together. Example: DQ3 (0.50) + DQ5 (0.45) = 0.50 * 0.45 = 0.225 effective multiplier.

two_required_skill_floor.rule: IF candidate scores 0 on two or more required_skills, THEN cap final_score at 0.40 regardless of other components
two_required_skill_floor._note: Prevents a candidate with excellent trajectory but zero retrieval and zero evaluation experience from sneaking into top 100.

ndcg_zone_strategy._meta: Scoring metric weights: NDCG@10 = 0.50, NDCG@50 = 0.30, MAP = 0.15, P@10 = 0.05. Half the grade is your top 10 alone.

top_10_to_20.treatment: Distinct high-scrutiny zone — run additional evidence extraction passes, re-check all 7 DQs, verify honeypot checks, manually review reasonings for factual accuracy
top_10_to_20._note: Getting rank 1-10 right is worth 10x what getting rank 40-50 right is worth. Treat this zone as its own sub-problem.

top_20_to_100.treatment: Standard pipeline output. Focus on correct DQ application and avoiding honeypots.

top_100_cutoff.treatment: Ensure no honeypots, no hard-reject DQ1 candidates, scores are monotonically non-increasing.

candidate_pool_intelligence._meta: Dataset-derived facts that should directly inform your architecture decisions, not just context.

tier_0_hard_eliminate_title.treatment: Title sieve. O(N). Eliminates before ANY expensive computation.

tier_1_generalist_tech.treatment: Hidden gem territory. Career description mining is decisive here, NOT title matching.
tier_1_generalist_tech._note: This is where the JD warning about 'keyword trap' bites hardest. A generalist tech person who shipped a recommendation system > an AI Research Engineer who only published papers.

tier_2_data_adjacent.treatment: Prime hidden gem territory. Check for ML/retrieval work in descriptions carefully.

tier_3_ml_ai_title.treatment: Direct pass with scrutiny. Apply all DQ checks. Do NOT reward title alone.

fictional_employer_warning.treatment: Correctly fall through to `unclassified` tier (neutral weight 0.75). DO NOT penalize. DO NOT attempt fuzzy matching to real companies.
fictional_employer_warning._CRITICAL: These are deliberate filler employers for the ~70K off-domain candidates. Their presence confirms company name is a WEAK signal for most of this pool.

high_leverage_employers._note: These are the single highest-signal employer matches for THIS specific JD. More relevant than FAANG (7-14 mentions each vs. 58-81 for these). From company_tiers.yaml ai_native section.
